member.name: Ms Polly Billington
member.synopsis: Ms Polly Billington is the Labour MP for East Thanet , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: DNACPR Decisions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will make an assessment of the potential merits of amending Do not attempt cardiopulmonary resuscitation forms to include a confirmation of consultation field that must be signed by the patie
writtenQuestions.answerText: No assessment of the potential merits of amending Do not attempt cardiopulmonary resuscitation (DNACPR) forms to include a confirmation of consultation field that must be signed by the patient or family has been undertaken.When a DNACPR decision is being ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: DNACPR Decisions: Learning Disability
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he has made an assessment of the potential merits of conducting a national audit of Do not attempt cardiopulmonary resuscitation orders for patients with learning disabilities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is clear that the inappropriate or blanket use of Do not attempt cardiopulmonary resuscitation (DNACPR) decisions is wholly unacceptable, and that no one should have a DNACPR decision in place simply because they have a learning disability....
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Non-crime Hate Incidents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, when her Department plans to publish the report on the College of Policing and National Police Chiefs’ Council review and recommendations on Non-Crime Hate Incidents.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The final report of the College of Policing and the National Police Chiefs’ Council Review of Non-Crime Hate Incidents is expected to be published in the coming weeks.The Government will set out its response to the Review’s recommendations at that time.

writtenQuestions.heading: Electrical Substations and Renewable Energy: Development Consent Orders
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what discussion he has had with National Grid Electricity Transmission on the siting of new substations and the consenting process for renewable projects under the Development Consent Order r
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Secretary of State has not held discussions with National Grid Electricity Transmission on the siting of new substations or the consenting process of renewable projects. In his quasi-judicial role in determining Nationally Significant Infrastructure P...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Batteries and Data Centres
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment his Department made of the potential merits of delaying connection offers for some protected battery projects and data centres to allow NESO and DESNZ to undertake other key a
writtenQuestions.answerText: NESO’s connections reforms have reduced the queue for generation and storage connections by over half, with the queue now largely in line with the capacity ranges set out in the Clean Power 2030 Action Plan. We are aware that the volume of batteries in th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Standing Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what discussions he has had with Ofgem about the balance between standing charges and unit rates for farmer and grower businesses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: My Rt. Hon. Friend the Secretary of State has regular discussions with Ofgem on a range of issues. Standing charges predominantly recover fixed costs that do not vary by energy use. This includes supplier’s operational costs and the cost of essential netw...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Israel: Palestine
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, whether she has had recent discussions with her Israeli counterpart on ending the detention of Palestinian children in Israeli prisons.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer of 16 September to Question 76963.

writtenQuestions.heading: Blood Cancer: Diagnosis
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to help increase early diagnosis for blood cancer.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department continues to support the National Health Service to diagnose and treat cancer, including blood cancers, as early and fast as possible.To increase early diagnosis of blood cancer, the NHS has implemented non-specific symptom (NSS) pathways f...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Nature Conservation: Job Creation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether his Department has made an estimate of the potential number of jobs that can be created from nature restoration projects.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to supporting job creation through nature restoration initiatives. In our policy-making process, we carefully consider a range of factors, including the potential for economic growth and employment. Our Nature for Climate Fund—...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Nature Conservation and Ecology: Technical Excellence Colleges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether her Department plans to include skills training for nature restoration and ecological programmes as part of the new Technical Excellence Colleges.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department is introducing Technical Excellence Colleges (TECs) to specialise in training skilled workforces which industry needs in priority sectors. This starts with the launch of ten construction TECs from September 2025. This will be followed by th...

writtenQuestions.heading: Art and Design: Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether her Department has made an assessment of the potential impact of art and design education on outcomes for students with SEND.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government established an independent Curriculum and Assessment Review in July 2024, covering ages 5 to 18. The Review seeks to deliver a rich, broad, inclusive and innovative curriculum that readies young people for life and work, including in arts s...

writtenQuestions.heading: Learning Disability: Primary Care
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether the Learning Disability annual health check has been removed as a national target for primary care in the 2025-26 NHS plan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The national target to complete annual health checks for 75% of people with a learning disability is not included in the 2025/26 NHS Planning Guidance. Most recent data from 2023/24 shows 79.6% of checks were delivered, surpassing the previous 75% Nationa...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Art and Design: Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether her Department has made an assessment of the potential impact of art and design education on the school-readiness of children entering primary school.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government’s Plan for Change sets out the department’s ambition for a record proportion of children starting school ready to learn. We will measure progress through 75% of children at the end of reception reaching a good level of development in the ea...

writtenQuestions.heading: Arts: Curriculum
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether the Enrichment Framework will (a) provide a minimum of 80 hours of enrichment across the academic year and (b) be in addition to curriculum time for arts subjects.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department, working closely with the Department for Culture, Media and Sport, has committed to publishing a new Enrichment Framework by the end of 2025. The framework is being developed alongside a working group consisting of experts from schools, you...

writtenQuestions.heading: Land Use
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether his Department plans to include a strategic nature restoration programme within the Land Use Framework.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is analysing responses to the consultation on Land Use in England. The Consultation focused on the need for a strategic spatial approach to nature restoration, emissions reduction, new homes, and infrastructure. Responses to the consultatio...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Israel: Military Aid
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, pursuant to the Answer of 11 June 2025 to Question 56822 on Israel: Military Aid, how many Israel Defense Forces personnel have been trained by the UK armed forces in the last 12 months.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK has a long history of providing assistance to other nations in the security and justice fields and continues to do so around the world. Fewer than ten IDF personnel have been trained in non-combat military courses in the UK in the last 12 months. T...

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Security Benefits: East Thanet
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, how many households who are receiving the maximum permitted benefits under the benefit cap have one or more children with a disability in East Thanet constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Households receiving disability benefits are exempt from the benefit cap. Households are exempt from the benefit cap if somebody in the household is receiving, for example, Disability Living Allowance or Personal Independence Payment. Universal Credit cus...

items.text: Name of donor: Parliamentary Renewable & Sustainable Energy Group (PRASEG) Secretariat for the APPG for Renewable & Sustainable Energy Address of donor: PRASEG, 10 Dean Farrar Street, London SW1H 0DX Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Accommodation, return flights and subsistence, value £808 Destination of visit: Denmark (Copenhagen) Dates of visit: 23 October 2025 to 24 October 2025 Purpose of visit: APPG visit to high-level meetings and site visits in Copenhagen cross-channel cooperation for decarbonising heat and energy. (Registered 14 November 2025)

Name of donor: Yachad Address of donor: Star House, 104-108 Grafton Road, London NW5 4BA Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights, accommodation, hospitality, travel, value £2,169 Destination of visit: Israel and Palestine (Various including Ramallah and Hebron) Dates of visit: 24 May 2026 to 28 May 2026 Purpose of visit: To meet with a wide range of individuals, public officials and organisations, as well as to visit locations where violence has taken place, to understand the steps needed to achieve a resolution to the Israel-Palestine conflict and to adequately respond to the humanitarian situation in Gaza. (Registered 5 June 2026)
